questions.questionText: Barlow and Ortolani tests are used for screening:
questions.optionB: Developmental dysplasia of the hip (DDH — Barlow provokes instability/dislocation; Ortolani reduces a dislocated hip — performed in all neonates)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Barlow (provocative) and Ortolani (reduction) tests screen for DDH in neonates. Why correct: DDH: spectrum from dysplasia to subluxation to dislocation. Barlow: adduction + posterior pressure → dislocatable hip gives a 'clunk' (provocation). Ortolani: abduction → dislocated hip reduces with a 'clunk' (reduction). Risk factors: female, breech, first-born, positive family history. Why A wrong: Clubfoot = clinical diagnosis (equinovarus deformity) — treated with Ponseti method. Why C wrong: Torticollis = head tilt, sternocleidomastoid contracture — clinical exam. Why D wrong: Scoliosis = Adam's forward bend test. Clinical relevance: USG of hips before 6 months (cartilaginous femoral head not visible on X-ray). After 6 months: X-ray (Perkin's, Hilgenreiner's, Shenton's lines). Treatment: Pavlik harness (0-6 months), closed reduction + spica cast (6-18 months), open reduction >18 months. Exam trap: Barlow = provocative (B for Bad, pushes OUT); Ortolani = reduces (O for On, puts back IN). Memory aid: 'Barlow = Bad (dislocates); Ortolani = OK (reduces).' Reference: Maheshwari – Congenital.
questions.conceptTag: DDH Barlow Ortolani screening Pavlik harness
